If journalists consistently ran away from scary things, there wouldn't be much journalism.
@Timcast I was asking why do you think that working more closely with locals / fixers for insight and safety "doesn't work"?
@TravelingAnna oooooh, yes!!

The book store has a community fridge they take care of, and when it runs low they post on IG/Twitter but have been struggling to get those posts through the algorithm, what if they had their own custom-made tool to track and alert their community

I went to a pottery studio recently and all the kiln firings were managed on a sign up sheet and receipts the owner had to tally at the end of the month: I wonder how quickly we could build a tool for him

I wanna do a livestream where I make people’s ideas for web-things as quickly as possible, but I want to find non-technical curious people with lots of ideas they think are impossible to build things for
@Timcast "That doesn't work" - why do you think that? Very serious Q that's at the heart of this I think
@Timcast so you don't think it's important to cover that story any more?
@Timcast I can imagine this situation being very different if you explained your fear and then asked locals for help or guidance.
@Timcast accommodation is different than respect, I think. Why make that video instead of working more cautiously, in hand with locals?
@Timcast if you felt it was dangerous why not work closer with locals / fixers? Be more cautious. But make a video like that? Come on man.
@Timcast the tweet I replied to which was about OWS and Milwaukee. 2 places where you antagonized community u should have been covering.
If you're not in the place where things are happening then you're not covering anything or servicing anyone @Timcast
